records.ety: [F. prlat, LL. praelatus, fr. L. praelatus, used as p. p. of praeferre to prefer, but from a different root. See Elate.]

defs: A clergyman of a superior order, as an archbishop or a bishop, having authority over the lower clergy; a dignitary of the church.
quotes: Hear him but reason in divinity, . . . You would desire the king were made a prelate.
notes: ☞ This word and the words derived from it are often used invidiously, in English ecclesiastical history, by dissenters, respecting the Established Church system.

quotes: Right prelating is busy laboring, and not lording.
